title: fragaria vesca subsp. elatior
intro: Fragaria vesca subsp. elatior, also known as the tall wood strawberry or alpine strawberry, is a subspecies of the wild strawberry plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Fragaria vesca subsp. elatior include its potential to support cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and act as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
